description: More than half, but not all, is 'shelami': the greater portion of an understood whole. The quantifier works with members or with an amount, and its claim leaves a remainder. 'sheloi' (MANY) only calls a quantity large; 'theula' (UNIV) includes the whole set.
articulatory_notes: Form 'sh' with the blade raised in the postalveolar zone; unvoiced friction opens onto front 'e' as the jaw opens halfway. Use the tip to touch the gumline and form 'l', then open fully for stressed central 'a'. Bring both lips together at resonant 'm' and release them toward high 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The opening echoes 'sheloi' (MANY), the other large-quantity form. After the shared hush and lateral contact, 'shelami' opens on stressed 'a' and closes at 'm' before 'i'; that gathering cadence can recall a portion becoming predominant.
usage_notes: The whole against which 'shelami' is measured may be explicit or understood from context. Its noun follows directly. A decision rule that gives the majority force requires its own claim.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: 'shelami' keeps most agreed distinct from all agreed, so a report does not erase the people who dissented.
